source: Official PoE patch notes and official forum Q&A

Definition.text: Brittle is an Ailment associated with cold damage that grants hits against afflicted enemies increased critical strike chance, with capped magnitude and duration-based effect.

Purpose.text: Increase critical hit probability against enemies affected by cold-related ailment application while limiting maximum effect and duration by design.

Core Rules.text: Cold-hit inflicted Brittle applies an increased critical strike chance against afflicted enemies. Magnitude is lower-capped relative to Shock/Chill; base values changed by patch and duration is patch-governed.

Internal Rules.text: GGG forum Q&A states the internal stored ailment values are scaled by 100 to preserve precision; user-facing logic can be represented as a 25 base coefficient formula.

Calculation Order.text: 1) cold damage from a hit calculates ailment magnitude against ailment threshold 2) apply Brittle formula coefficient 25 * (Damage / AilmentThreshold)^0.4 3) cap by versioned maximum critical chance bonus 4) apply duration at current patch base.

Priority Rules.text: Brittle is an alternate ailment with independent magnitude-cap behavior; modifiers to Critical Strike Chance scale final critical chance but do not replace the ailment magnitude cap model.

Preconditions.text: A cold hit must inflict the Brittle ailment; patch-specific minimum/threshold behavior from ailment-threshold mechanics applies.

Postconditions.text: Until Brittle expires, all Hit resolution on afflicted targets must account for the current Brittle critical strike chance bonus when evaluating critical strike chance.

Limitations.text: Alternate-ailment model includes lower magnitude caps than Shock/Chill. Short-duration and edge interactions are patch-specific and can be patched in bug-fix updates.

Caps.text: Current major documented cap is up to +15% critical strike chance (3.10 baseline), with subsequent patch reversions to smaller values in later versions noted in history.

Formula.text: BrittleMagnitude is approximately 25 * (Damage / AilmentThreshold)^0.4, with internal value stored at 100x for precision; displayed critical strike chance bonus uses the scaled user-facing value and is clamped to versioned cap.

Conflicts.text: Early alternate-ailment interactions had bug states with Yoke of Suffering and ailment grouping semantics; fixed in 3.9 patch notes.

Exceptions.text: 3.21.1 changed ground effect preservation timing by allowing non-Freezing elemental ailments below 300ms to persist.

Hidden Rules.text: For internal precision, internal magnitude is stored scaled by 100; this is not a visible player-facing decimal but affects exact rounding behavior near thresholds.

Developer Notes.text: Brittle was introduced as part of the 3.5.0 alternate ailment expansion (Sap/Scorch/Brittle) and its behavior has since been tuned through patch-specific cap updates.

Historical Changes.text: Introduced in 3.5.0; 3.6.0 clarified bug fix where +critical chance interacted with increased Critical Strike Chance modifiers; 3.9.0 introduced alternate-ailment lower caps; 3.10.0 raised max effect to +15%; 3.19.0 reduced baseline to +6%; 3.21.1 fixed short-duration discard behavior for elemental ailments.

text.note: Introduced as a new ailment (alternate ailment set with Sap and Scorch).
Now grants up to +10% crit chance on hit; bonus now properly interacts with increased critical strike chance modifiers.
Alternate ailments (including Brittle) use lower cap mechanics rather than Shock/Chill defaults.
Brittle maximum increased to up to +15%; base duration set to 4 seconds.
Default value and cap behavior changed: up to +6% from previous +15%.
Elemental ailments other than Freeze are not discarded if duration is lower than 300ms.

text: A high cold-damage hit can apply higher Brittle magnitude up to the current patch cap.
Observed implementation note: internal scaling uses a stored 100x value for fractional precision.
Brittle is not equal to a fixed critical chance increase; magnitude depends on cold hit size versus ailment threshold and is capped.
Brittle values are not computed with the same 50 coefficient as Shock; formula differs.

modifies: critical strike chance against afflicted enemies
cannot_affect: non-crit chance calculations that ignore target ailment state
